sections.paragraph:
fut. יִטֶּה, apoc. יֵט, וַיֵּט, וַיֶּט־.

(1) to stretch out, to extend (Arab. نطا to stretch out threads; cognate words are נָתַח, מָתַח, לָתַח which see).

(a) to stretch out, to extend (ausftrecken), e.g. the hand, Exo 8:2, 13 10:12 , 21 Exo 10:21often used of the hand of God in threatening, Jer 51:25 Eze 6:14 14:9 , 13 Eze 14:13 Isa 5:25 or of a man assailing God, Job 15:25 also a spear, Jos 8:18 a measuring line (followed by עַל, die Meßfchnur an etwas legen), Job 38:5 Isa 44:13 Lam 2:8 an ambush, a metaphor taken from nets, Psa 21:12 also, to extend, to elongate (ausdehnen), to draw out by extending; Isa 3:16, נְטוּיֹות גָּרֹון “with a stretched out (or an erect) neck;” Psa 102:12, צֵל נָטוּי “an elongated shadow;” i.e. having become longer at evening; comp. Psa 109:23.

(b) to stretch, to unfold (ausbreiten ausfpannen), e.g. a tent, Gen 12:8 26:25 , heaven; Isa 40:22, הַנֹּוטֶה כַדֹּק שָׁמַיִם “who spreadeth out the heaven as a curtain;” 1Ch 21:10, שָׁלֹושׁ אֲנִי נֹטֶה עָלֶיךָ “I spread out to thee three things,” i.e. I propose them to thee, choose one; compare 2Sa 24:12 (where for נֹוטֶה there is נֹוטֵל).

(c) intrans. to spread selves out (e.g. flocks of any one in a land), Job 15:29.

(2) to incline, to bow, e.g. the shoulder, Gen 49:15 the heart, Psa 119:12 the heaven (spoken of God), Psa 18:10 to cast down (enemies), Psa 17:11. Part. pass., Psa 62:4, קִיר נָטוּי “a wall inclined,” ready to fall; intrans. used of feet inclining, ready to fall, Psa 73:2 of the day as declining, Jud. 19:8 of the shadow of a dial moving downwards, 2Ki 20:10.

(3) to turn, to turn away, to turn to one side, Isa 66:12, “behold I will turn peace upon her as a river.” Gen 39:21, וַיֵּט אֵלָיו חֶסֶד “and he turned mercy upon him,” i.e. conciliated favour to him; more often intrans. Num 20:17 22:23 26:33 followed by אֶל (to some one), Gen 38:16 followed by מִן and מֵעִם from any thing (as from a way), Psa 44:19 119:51 , 157 Psa 119:157 Job 31:7 1Ki 11:9 followed by אַחֲרֵי to turn on any one’s side or part, Exo 23:2 Jud. 9:3 1Ki 2:28 comp. 1Sa 8:3. Hence-

(4) to go away, 1Sa 14:7.

Niphal, pass. of Kal No. 1, to be stretched out (as a measuring line), Zec 1:16 to spread itself (a river), Num 24:6 to elongate itself (the shadow of the evening), Jer 6:4 compare Virg. Ecl., i. 84.

Hiphil, הִטָּה fut. יַטֶּה, apoc. יַט, יָט, וַיַּט 2Sa 19:15 imp. apoc. הַט Psa 17:6

(1) i.q. Kal No. 1 (though not so much used)-(a) to extend, to stretch out, e.g. the hand, Isa 31:3 Jer 6:12 15:6 one’s members on a couch Amo 2:8.-(b) to expand, as a curtain, Isa 54:2 2Sa 21:10 a tent, 2Sa 16:22.

(2) to incline (downwards), Gen 24:14 Psa 144:5 (God bowing the heavens); specially, the ear, Jer 7:24, 26 11:8 followed by לְ to some one, Psa 17:6 31:3 71:2 78:1 116:2 Pro 4:20 5:1 .

(3) to turn, to turn away, to turn (to one side), i.q. Kal No. 3, Num 22:23 2Sa 3:27 as-

(a) any one’s heart, 2Sa 19:15 followed by אֶל, לְ to any one, to wisdom, 1Ki 8:58 Pro 2:2 21:1 Psa 119:36 141:4 followed by אַחֲרֵי 1Ki 11:2 in a bad sense, to seduce, Pro 7:21 Isa 44:20.-(b) הִטָּה חֶסֶד עַל פּ׳ to turn favour to some one, i.e. to conciliate favour for him, Ezr 7:28 9:9 compare Kal, Gen 39:21.-(c) to turn aside, i.e. to avert evil, Jer 5:25.-(d) intens. to push out of the way, Job 24:4 comp. Amo 2:7.-(e) to repel, (to give refusal to a petitioner), Psa 27:9.-(f) intrans. to deflect, to decline, Job 23:11 Isa 30:11 Psa 125:5.-(g) הִטָּה מִשְׁפָּט 1Sa 8:3 and followed by gen., Exo 23:6 Deu 27:19 Lam 3:35, to turn aside any one’s right in judgment; without the noun, Exo 23:2, לִנְטֹת אַחֲרֵי רַבִּים לְהַטֹּת “to follow many to turn aside,” i.e. wrest (judgment); also followed by an acc. of pers., to turn any one aside, i.e. to turn aside his right; Pro 18:5 Isa 10:2 29:21 Amo 5:12.

Derivatives מִטָּה, מַטֶּה, מֻטֶּה, מֻטָּה and the pr.n. יֻטָּה
